step_by_step_thinking:
Answer:

The average diameter of the apical foramen in young adults (18–25 years) is typically around **500 micrometers**. This value reflects normal anatomical variation, with studies showing that younger individuals tend to have slightly smaller apices compared to older adults due to physiological changes over time.
